name: Zenlink
description: Zenlink is an underlying cross-chain DEX protocol based on Polkadot, and is committed to become the DEX composable hub of Polkadot. By accessing the ultimate, open and universal cross-chain DEX protocol based on Substrate, Zenlink DEX Protocol enables all parachains to build DEX and achieve liquidity sharing in one click. Zenlink DEX Protocol includes Module, WASM, and EVM implementations, which are flexible and adaptable, allowing for customizable compositions and interoperability with different DeFi modules.
policy:
### Project Overview
Zenlink DEX Protocol is the underlying unified and universal cross-chain DEX Protocol, which enables parachains to quickly have DEX functionality and share liquidity with other parachains. In addition, Zenlink DEX Aggregator can connect to all DEX DApps on Polkadot, which together form the Zenlink DEX Network with full liquidity.

name: Bitget
description:
Launched in 2018, Bitget provides comprehensive and secure trading solutions to our global userbase.
The following is a description of the types of vulnerabilities we accept, and we reject software bugs that do not pose a security risk.
Received vulnerability types:
- Authentication and authorization issues: including password reset, session management, access control, etc.
- Input validation and data processing: Issues involving user input, such as cross-site scripting (XSS), SQL injection, remote code execution, etc.
- Sensitive information leakage: including unencrypted transmission, incorrect data storage, etc. that lead to sensitive information leakage.
- Network security configuration: involves configuration issues such as network protocols, firewalls, and security groups.
- Logic vulnerability: involves a logic error or inconsistency in an application that may result in unauthorized access or disruption of functionality.
- Server configuration: including network server, database server and other configuration issues.
- Mobile Application Security: Covers vulnerabilities in mobile applications such as insecure data storage, unauthorized access, etc.
Unreceived software bug types:
- Interface issues, style issues, or user experience issues.
- Performance issues, memory leaks, or optimization issues.
- Feature bugs or feature suggestions.
- Vulnerabilities that have been publicly disclosed and do not pose a security risk.
policy:
# Bitget Bug Bounty Program

## 1. Project Overview

Established in 2018, Bitget is the world's leading cryptocurrency exchange and Web3 company. Serving over 25 million users in 100+ countries and regions, the Bitget exchange is committed to helping users trade smarter with its pioneering copy trading feature and other trading solutions. Formerly known as BitKeep, Bitget Wallet is a world-class multi-chain crypto wallet that offers an array of comprehensive Web3 solutions and features including...
